what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter ledger entry that records an image attachment in the design-assets channel, pointing to the image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Gold_Web target.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter with no body content.
Fields include id att-7869d950, channel design-assets, attachment_type images, target image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Gold_Web, and an added timestamp of 2026-06-22T12:26:23.492415+00:00.
The target uses an image:// URI scheme rather than a filesystem path.
There are no code symbols present.
standouts: References a specific icon asset via an image:// URI.
Typed as an images attachment in the design-assets channel.
conventions: Follows an att- prefixed identifier convention.
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.
Uses a URI-style target pointer for the attached resource.
concerns: The attachment has no human-readable title, caption, or name.
The target URI provides no extension, version, or resolution hint.
There is no author or modification history field.
notes: A minimal, timestamped channel attachment record that links a design-assets entry to a specific icon image via a custom URI scheme.
questions: What does Icon_Gold_Web depict and where is the actual image file stored?
Is image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Gold_Web resolvable by the consuming system?
Who added this attachment and what prompted its addition?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is meant to record an image attachment for a design-assets channel entry, and it does exactly that.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, typed link between a channel memory entry and a named icon image asset so the attachment can be discovered and resolved later.
novelty: The use of an image:// URI scheme as the attachment target is a distinct convention compared to typical file paths or URLs.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed pointer that connects a channel memory entry to a named icon image asset in the design-assets channel, supporting asset discovery and traceability.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment ledger entry in the design-assets channel that records an images attachment pointing to image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Gold_Web with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register and link a specific icon image asset to a channel memory entry for later retrieval or reference.
